title: Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza 2026: Toppings, Price, Calories & Honest Review
excerpt: Full guide to the sweet and smoky Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza. Exact prices, calorie comparisons, ingredient breakdown, and best crust choices for 2026.
content:
BBQ pizza is the most divisive item on any pizza menu. Half the room loves the sweet-smoky sauce swap. The other half refuses to accept anything except tomato. Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza sits squarely in the middle of that debate — and does it better than almost any competitor in the US market. In 2026, it remains one of the top five most-ordered specialty pizzas at Papa Johns, driven by a combination of genuinely quality ingredients: all-white-meat grilled chicken, hickory-smoked bacon, fresh-cut red onions, and a proprietary BBQ sauce base that manages to be tangy and rich without becoming syrupy.

In a three-chain taste test published by a food review site comparing Papa Johns, Domino's, and Pizza Hut BBQ chicken specialty pizzas, Papa Johns' BBQ Chicken pizza was called a "standout, blending sweet and savory tastes perfectly." That kind of feedback is rare when the other two chains are directly in the comparison.

This guide gives you the complete 2026 menu & price picture: every price by size, the full calorie and nutrition breakdown per slice, a detailed ingredient breakdown, crust recommendations for this specific pizza, the key differences from the Hawaiian BBQ Chicken variant, and an honest review of where this pizza earns its price. You can also view our full current menu prices here.

What Is Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza?

Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza is a specialty pizza that uses BBQ sauce as its base instead of the chain's standard vine-ripened tomato sauce. Over that sauce goes grilled white-meat chicken, hickory-smoked bacon strips, fresh-cut red onions, and 100% real mozzarella cheese — all on the signature hand-tossed never-frozen original dough.

The flavor profile is deliberately sweet and smoky: the BBQ sauce provides tangy sweetness, the bacon brings smokiness and salt, the grilled chicken adds a clean protein base, and the red onions contribute a mild pungency that brightens the entire combination. Together these create a pizza that is noticeably different in character from a standard tomato-based specialty pizza — which is exactly the point.

What distinguishes the Papa Johns version from Domino's BBQ Chicken specialty and Pizza Hut's equivalent is the quality of the chicken. Papa Johns uses all-white-meat grilled chicken — not breaded, not pre-cooked popcorn chicken pieces — and the chicken is seasoned before grilling. This results in chunks that hold their moisture through oven baking without becoming rubbery, a challenge that cheaper chicken toppings consistently fail, as backed up by information on their official website.

As with all Papa Johns pizzas, every box includes one Special Garlic Dipping Sauce and one Pepperoncini pepper. For this pizza specifically, ranch dipping sauce is the preferred upgrade — the cool creaminess of ranch against the smoky BBQ pizza is one of the best flavor combinations on the menu.

Complete Toppings List — Every Ingredient Explained

  • BBQ Sauce (base): Papa Johns' proprietary BBQ sauce is made from tomato paste, molasses, sugar, vinegar, and spices. It is tangy and moderately sweet — not as sweet as some fast-food BBQ sauces, but noticeably sweeter than the standard pizza sauce. The molasses base gives it a slight smokiness even before the bacon is added.
  • Grilled White-Meat Chicken: All-white-meat grilled chicken, seasoned and cooked before application. No breading, no fillers. Arrives on the pizza as identifiable chunks rather than pre-shredded or diced pieces. This is the single most important quality differentiator between Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza and lower-tier versions of this pizza.
  • Hickory-Smoked Bacon: Bacon strips (not crumbles or bits) with a hickory smoke profile that complements the BBQ sauce base. Contributes both fat and salt, which balances the sweetness of the sauce. The strips crisp partially in the oven, adding a textural element that crumbled bacon cannot provide.
  • Fresh-cut Red Onions: Thinly sliced red onions added fresh before baking. Red onions are more assertive than white onions and hold up better through the oven bake without becoming completely soft. They add a mild sharpness that cuts through the richness of the bacon and BBQ sauce.
  • 100% Real Mozzarella Cheese: The same real mozzarella cheese used across all Papa Johns pizzas. On a BBQ base, the mild, slightly milky flavor of real mozzarella works better than a sharper cheese would — it acts as a neutral layer between the sweet sauce and the smoky meats.
  • Optional regional addition: Some Papa Johns locations add a light drizzle of extra BBQ sauce on top of the cheese. This is not standard but can be requested when ordering — it intensifies the BBQ flavor without overwhelming the bacon.

Prices reflect the national standard. Local variation of $0.50–$2.00 is common. Delivery fees are not included.

  • Apply verified promo codes from our Papa Johns Coupons page — deals updated daily.

BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza is nutritionally different from tomato-based specialty pizzas primarily because of the BBQ sauce. The sauce contains added sugar (from molasses) which adds approximately 3–5 grams of sugar per slice compared to the standard tomato sauce. It is also slightly lower in sodium than meat-heavy pizzas like The Works because the chicken is leaner than sausage and Canadian bacon. Which Crust Is Best for BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza?

Crust choice changes the BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza experience meaningfully because the BBQ sauce is sweeter and wetter than tomato sauce, which affects how different crusts hold up.

  • Original Crust — best choice for delivery. The hand-tossed dough is substantial enough to absorb moisture from the BBQ sauce without becoming soggy by the time the pizza reaches you.
  • Crispy Thin Crust — order carryout only. The Thin Crust is delicious when fresh but struggles with the moisture content of BBQ sauce during delivery.
  • New York Style — excellent for groups. Larger slices with a pliable crust make this a strong choice for sharing.
  • Epic Stuffed Crust — the premium choice. Mozzarella baked into the crust edge pairs surprisingly well with the BBQ flavor.

Honest Review: Is Papa Johns BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza Worth Ordering in 2026?

What customers consistently praise:
The all-white-meat grilled chicken is the most frequently cited strength — it distinguishes this pizza from cheaper versions at competitors where the chicken is pre-processed. The BBQ sauce quality is genuine: molasses-based with real tang rather than the corn-syrup sweetness of generic fast-food BBQ. The hickory-smoked bacon strips add both flavor and texture that bacon crumbles cannot match, a point echoed by many reviewers on YouTube pizza reviews.

Where it falls short:
The BBQ sauce sweetness can be polarizing, particularly on reheated slices where the sugar in the sauce intensifies. If you are reheating leftovers, use an oven or air fryer rather than a microwave — microwaving concentrates the sauce sweetness.

The verdict: At $20.99 for a Large, BBQ Chicken Bacon Pizza is the same price as The Works despite having fewer toppings — the premium reflects the cost of quality grilled chicken versus sausage and Canadian bacon. For customers who specifically want BBQ flavor in their pizza, it is the best option available from a major US chain in 2026. Looking for sides to go with it? We recommend standard Garlic Parmesan Breadsticks to offset the sweetness!

title: Papa Johns Pan Pizza 2026: Price, Toppings, Calories & Honest Review
excerpt: Papa Johns Pan Pizza is back in 2026 with 6 cheeses & garlic parmesan crust. Full review, price ($11.99), calories per slice, toppings list & Pizza Hut comparison.
content:
What Is Papa Johns Pan Pizza?

Papa Johns Pan Pizza is a thick-crust pizza baked directly in a metal pan, which gives it a completely different texture from the Original Crust Classic Pizzas or New York Style options. The crust is fluffy and airy on the inside, with a crispy, caramelized bottom created by baking Parmesan cheese and Papa Johns Special Garlic Sauce directly into the pan before the dough goes in.

The result is a pizza that has three distinct texture layers:

  • Top layer: A six-cheese blend spread edge-to-edge, creating a crispy cheese "crown" along the edges — similar to Detroit-style pizza
  • Middle layer: Soft, pillowy dough with an open crumb structure that does not feel heavy or doughy
  • Bottom layer: A golden, caramelized crust infused with garlic parmesan — no greasy oil-soaked base

This is also not a limited-time offer. Papa Johns has confirmed Pan Pizza as a permanent addition to their core menu — though location availability may vary. Find your nearest store using our Papa Johns Store Locator.

Papa Johns Pan Pizza Price (2026)

SizeToppingsStarting Price
Medium (12 inch)1 topping$11.99
Medium (12 inch)Additional toppingsVaries by location

Important pricing notes:

  • Pan Pizza is currently available in medium only (12 inches, 8 slices). No large or small size exists yet.
  • The $11.99 price is the national suggested retail price. Your local store may charge slightly more or less.
  • Best way to save: Apply a promo code at checkout. Check our Papa Johns Coupons page for verified deals updated daily.
  • Papa Rewards members earn Papa Dough on every Pan Pizza order. Join Papa Rewards free here.

The 6-Cheese Blend: What Makes It Different

Every other pan pizza at major chains uses mozzarella as the primary cheese. Papa Johns Pan Pizza uses a six-cheese blend that covers the pizza from edge to edge:

CheeseFlavor ProfileRole in the Blend
MozzarellaMild, creamyStretch and base melt
ProvoloneSlightly tangyAdded depth and pull
FontinaRich, butteryCreaminess and richness
AsiagoSharp, complexFlavor intensity
ParmesanSalty, nuttyCrust bottom caramelization
RomanoBold, agedFinishing sharpness

Because the cheese blend reaches all the way to the edge, it develops a caramelized cheese "crown" along the crust. No artificial colors or flavors are used — consistent with Papa Johns' "Better Ingredients. Better Pizza." promise.

Papa Johns Pan Pizza Toppings — Complete List

Pan Pizza can hold up to 7 toppings, and the thick crust supports them without becoming soggy. All standard Papa Johns toppings are available:

Meat toppings: Pepperoni, Italian Sausage, Bacon, Canadian Bacon, Grilled Chicken, Beef, Anchovies

Vegetable toppings: Onions, Green Peppers, Mushrooms, Black Olives, Roma Tomatoes, Banana Peppers, Jalapeños, Fresh Spinach, Roasted Garlic

Sauce options: Original Pizza Sauce, BBQ Sauce, Garlic Parmesan Sauce, Buffalo Sauce, Spinach Alfredo

💡 Tip: The garlic parmesan bottom crust is baked in by default and cannot be removed. If you prefer milder flavor, choose Original Pizza Sauce as your base. For the complete Papa Johns menu with prices, see our full guide.

Honest Review: What Real Customers Are Saying in 2026

What people love

The caramelized bottom crust is universally praised. The combination of garlic sauce and Parmesan baked into the pan creates a flavor that reviewers compared to Cheez-It crackers — salty, sharp, and deeply savory. One major food review site gave it a rare 10 out of 10.

The cheese crown along the border is a standout feature, especially compared to Pizza Hut's plain crust edge. The dough texture is praised for being airy without feeling undercooked.

What people do not love

Location inconsistency is the most common complaint — some stores execute it better than others. Medium-only size also frustrates customers ordering for groups. For group orders, pair it with our Papa Johns Sides & Dips guide to round out the meal.

Papa Johns Pan Pizza vs Pizza Hut vs Domino's

How to Order Papa Johns Pan Pizza

  • Online: Visit papajohns.com and apply a promo code at checkout
  • Papa Johns App: Get exclusive app-only deals and earn Papa Rewards on every order
  • Carryout: Find your store using our Papa Johns Store Locator — avoids delivery fees

💡 Pro tip: Order carryout for the crispiest bottom crust. If reheating, use an air fryer at 350°F for 3–4 minutes.

Final Verdict: Should You Order It?

Papa Johns Pan Pizza in 2026 is a genuinely impressive product. The six-cheese blend, caramelized garlic parmesan bottom crust, and edge-to-edge coverage give it real differentiation from Pizza Hut and Domino's. At $11.99 for a medium, the value is solid. If your first experience was flat, try carryout at a different location — because when it is made correctly, this pizza delivers on its promise.

title: Papa Johns Wings: All Flavors, Prices & Calories (2026 Complete Guide)
excerpt: Every Papa Johns wing flavor ranked in 2026   Buffalo, BBQ, Honey Chipotle, Garlic Parmesan & more. Bone in vs boneless, exact prices, calories & best dips guide.
content:
Papa Johns built its reputation on pizza but ask any regular and they will tell you the wings are just as much of a reason to order. Papa Johns wings are oven-baked, made with all-white meat chicken, and available in more flavor combinations than most people realize. They are not an afterthought on this menu.

In this complete 2026 guide, we cover every Papa Johns wing flavor, the bone-in vs boneless comparison, exact prices, calories per wing, the best dipping sauce pairings, and how to get the most value on every wing order.

Papa Johns Wings Quick Overview

Papa Johns offers two types of wings traditional bone-in wings and premium all-white-meat boneless wings. Both are oven-baked (not fried), which gives them a slightly different texture than traditional deep-fried wings leaner, with a cleaner flavor that lets the sauce shine through.

Available wing flavors in 2026:

  • Buffalo (Classic)
  • BBQ
  • Honey Chipotle
  • Garlic Parmesan
  • Spicy Garlic
  • Original (unsauced)

All Papa Johns Wing Flavors Ranked & Reviewed

1. Buffalo Wings The Classic 🏆

Heat Level: Medium-High | Best Dip: Blue Cheese or Ranch

Buffalo is the definitive Papa Johns wing flavor (inspired by authentic Buffalo wing tradition) and their consistent top-seller. The sauce is tangy, vinegary, and buttery with a genuine heat that builds across a few wings not a mild, watered-down version. Papa Johns gets the Buffalo balance right by keeping the sauce sharp and the heat real without making it too intense for people who aren't hardcore heat lovers.

Bone-in vs Boneless on Buffalo: Bone-in wins here. The richness of the dark meat amplifies the tangy buffalo sauce in a way boneless white meat simply doesn't match.

Best pairing: Blue Cheese Sauce the creamy, tangy sharpness of the blue cheese is the traditional Buffalo wing pairing and it works perfectly. Ranch is a good alternative if you prefer something milder.

Rating: 5/5 The benchmark. Order this first if you haven't tried Papa Johns wings before.

2. Honey Chipotle Wings The Surprise Favorite

Heat Level: Medium | Best Dip: Ranch

Honey Chipotle is frequently cited as the best flavor on the Papa Johns wing menu by customers who discover it, and it is genuinely excellent. The combination of sweet honey and smoky chipotle creates a complex, layered flavor that is more interesting than straight Buffalo or BBQ. The heat level is medium present but not aggressive which makes it accessible to a wider range of tastes.

Why it stands out: The sweetness from the honey balances the smokiness of the chipotle in a way that makes each wing feel genuinely satisfying. The sauce clings well to both bone-in and boneless versions.

Rating: 4.8/5 Arguably the most underrated flavor on the menu. Order this for something beyond the standard Buffalo.

3. Garlic Parmesan Wings For Non-Spicy Fans

Heat Level: None | Best Dip: Ranch or Special Garlic Sauce

Garlic Parmesan is the go-to choice for anyone who loves wings but does not enjoy heat. The sauce is rich, creamy, buttery, and deeply garlicky with real parmesan adding a sharp, nutty edge. It is genuinely one of the most flavorful non-spicy wing options you will find at a pizza chain.

Why it stands out: The Garlic Parmesan sauce is made with similar quality to Papa Johns' famous Special Garlic Sauce deep, aromatic garlic flavor with a richness that coats every wing generously. No heat at all, but maximum flavor.

Rating: 4.5/5 Best choice for non-spicy wing lovers. Also the best option for kids.

4. BBQ Wings Sweet and Smoky

Heat Level: None | Best Dip: Ranch or Honey Mustard

BBQ wings are a reliable, crowd-pleasing choice that works well for groups where not everyone wants spice. The Papa Johns BBQ sauce is sweet and thick with a good smoky undertone slightly sweeter than the chipotle version but without any of the heat. It clings well and gives a glossy coating on both bone-in and boneless.

Rating: 4.2/5 Safe, solid, and crowd-pleasing. Not the most exciting flavor but consistently good.

5. Spicy Garlic Wings Heat Meets Garlic

Heat Level: Medium-High | Best Dip: Ranch

Spicy Garlic combines the aromatic depth of Papa Johns' garlic sauce with a genuine heat kick from jalapeño and red pepper. If you enjoy both garlic flavor and real heat, this is the wing for you. The spice level is comparable to Buffalo possibly slightly higher with the garlic adding an extra flavor dimension.

Rating: 4.3/5 Excellent for spice lovers who want something beyond standard Buffalo.

6. Original (Unsauced) Wings

Heat Level: None | Best Dip: Any sauce you choose

The Original wings are lightly seasoned and served without any sauce coating designed for customers who want to apply their own dipping sauce or who prefer a cleaner wing flavor. The oven-baked chicken quality comes through clearly without any sauce competing with it.

Rating: 3.8/5 Good base quality but benefits significantly from a dipping sauce.

Bone-In vs Boneless Wings Which Should You Order?

This is the most common Papa Johns wing question, and the honest answer depends on what you prioritize. For maximum flavor and the most satisfying wing experience, bone-in wins. For groups where ease of eating matters especially with kids boneless is the better choice.

Bone-in wings offer a richer, more complex flavor thanks to the dark and white meat combination, but are slightly higher in price. Boneless wings are cleaner, leaner all-white meat and easier to eat, commonly paired well with BBQ or Honey Chipotle.

Papa Johns Wings Calories (2026)

Here are the approximate calories per wing. Remember that full orders typically come in 8, 16, or 24 pieces.

  • Original (unsauced): ~70 cal (Bone-In) | ~60 cal (Boneless)
  • Garlic Parmesan: ~90 cal (Bone-In) | ~80 cal (Boneless)
  • BBQ: ~80 cal (Bone-In) | ~75 cal (Boneless)
  • Buffalo: ~75 cal (Bone-In) | ~70 cal (Boneless)
  • Honey Chipotle: ~85 cal (Bone-In) | ~80 cal (Boneless)
  • Spicy Garlic: ~80 cal (Bone-In) | ~72 cal (Boneless)

For full nutrition details including sodium and protein content, see our Papa Johns Nutrition Guide.

Best Dipping Sauces for Papa Johns Wings

Papa Johns offers 9 dipping sauces. Here is exactly which sauce goes best with each wing flavor:

  • Buffalo: Blue Cheese or Ranch
  • Honey Chipotle: Ranch
  • Garlic Parmesan: Ranch or Garlic Sauce
  • BBQ: Honey Mustard
  • Spicy Garlic: Ranch
  • Original: Special Garlic Sauce

How to Order Papa Johns Wings for Maximum Value

1. Check for combo deals first. Papa Johns frequently bundles wings with a large pizza at a combo price. Before ordering wings separately, check the current deals page combo savings can be significant.

2. Order 16 pieces for groups. The price per wing drops on the 16-piece order compared to two 8-piece orders. For groups of 3 or more, the 16-piece is always better value.

3. Use Papa Rewards. Every wing order earns Papa Dough points toward free food. If you order wings regularly, joining Papa Rewards is a free and straightforward way to earn toward future orders.

Papa Johns Wings vs Dominos vs Pizza Hut

Papa Johns wings are competitive on quality the Honey Chipotle and Garlic Parmesan flavors are genuinely superior to most competitor equivalents. The free Garlic Sauce inclusion is also a notable advantage. Domino's has a slight edge on raw flavor variety, but Papa Johns wins on quality per flavor.

Final Verdict Are Papa Johns Wings Worth Ordering?

Yes consistently. The oven-baked approach keeps the quality clean and lets the sauces do the work, which Papa Johns executes well across their full flavor lineup. Start with the Buffalo if you want the classic experience. Try the Honey Chipotle if you want to discover the best-kept secret on the menu. Order Garlic Parmesan if anyone at the table doesn't eat spicy food it is the best non-heat option available.

title: Papa Johns Desserts Menu: Every Sweet Treat, Price & Calorie (2026)
excerpt: Discover every Papa Johns dessert in 2026   Chocolate Chip Cookie, Double Chocolate Brownie, Cinnamon Pull Aparts & Oreo Papa Bites. Prices, calories & honest reviews.
content:
Most people order Papa John's for the pizza. But here's what the regulars know: the desserts are just as good as the main event. The Chocolate Chip Cookie alone has developed a cult following that rivals the Pepperoni Pizza in terms of repeat orders and for good reason.

In this complete 2026 guide, we break down every Papa Johns dessert the Chocolate Chip Cookie, Double Chocolate Chip Brownie, Cinnamon Pull Aparts, and Oreo Cookie Papa Bites with full prices, calories, honest reviews, and the best ways to order them. Whether you're finishing a family pizza night or just need something sweet, this guide tells you everything before you order.

What Makes Papa Johns Desserts Different?

Papa John's applies the same "Better Ingredients" philosophy to their desserts that they use for their pizzas. Every dessert is:

  • Baked fresh never frozen or pre-made off-site
  • Made with real ingredients no artificial flavors in standard recipes
  • Served warm timed to come out of the oven with your pizza order
  • Shareable portions designed for groups, though solo ordering is completely valid

The result is a dessert lineup that consistently outperforms expectations for a pizza chain. These are not afterthought menu fillers they are genuinely good baked goods.

*Prices may vary slightly by location. Always check your local Papa Johns app for current pricing.

1. Papa Johns Chocolate Chip Cookie Full Review

Price: $7.29 | Calories: ~190 cal per slice | Serves: 2–4 people

If there is one Papa Johns dessert that every first-timer should try, it is the Chocolate Chip Cookie. This is not a standard cookie it is a large, family-sized skillet-style cookie baked fresh and cut into 8 slices, served warm with gooey melted chocolate chips throughout.

What It Tastes Like

The texture sits somewhere between a classic cookie and a soft cookie cake slightly crisp on the very outer edge, soft and chewy through the middle, with pockets of melted semi-sweet chocolate chips in every bite. The cookie is buttery and rich without being overwhelming, and the size means each slice is substantial enough to satisfy a genuine dessert craving.

Why People Love It

It arrives warm. That is genuinely the secret the chocolate chips are still melted and gooey when the cookie comes out of the oven, which creates an entirely different experience from a pre-baked or refrigerated cookie. Combined with the soft, fresh-baked texture, it is hard to beat as a dessert for sharing at a pizza night.

Best Way to Order It

The Chocolate Chip Cookie pairs perfectly with a vanilla ice cream scoop if you have some at home. Order it as part of a Papa Pairings deal if available combining a large pizza and a cookie frequently offers savings over ordering separately.

Nutrition Breakdown (per slice, large cookie):

Nutrient Amount
Calories~190 cal
Fat~9g
Carbohydrates~26g
Sugar~15g
Protein~2g

Allergens: Contains wheat, dairy, eggs, soy. Not gluten-free.

2. Papa Johns Double Chocolate Chip Brownie Full Review

Price: $7.29 | Calories: ~240 cal per square | Serves: 2–4 people

The Double Chocolate Chip Brownie is for serious chocolate lovers. Where the Chocolate Chip Cookie is sweet and buttery, the brownie is dense, fudgy, and intensely chocolatey a completely different eating experience on the same dessert menu.

What It Tastes Like

Each order comes as 9 individual brownie squares soft and slightly cakey on the outside, warm and fudgy in the center, packed with semi-sweet chocolate chips throughout. The "double chocolate" comes from both chocolate in the batter and the extra chocolate chips baked in, creating a rich, layered chocolate flavor that genuine chocolate lovers will appreciate immediately.

Why People Love It

It is the most indulgent item on the Papa Johns dessert menu. If the Chocolate Chip Cookie is the safe, crowd-pleasing choice, the Double Chocolate Chip Brownie is the choice for people who want maximum chocolate in every bite. The 9-square format also makes it easy to share without anyone feeling shortchanged.

Best Way to Order It

Order it fresh and eat it warm the brownie texture is at its absolute best within the first 20 minutes after baking. If you have leftovers, reheat in a conventional oven at 300°F for 5 minutes to restore the warm, fudgy center. Microwaving works in a pinch but can make the texture rubbery.

Nutrition Breakdown (per square):

Nutrient Amount
Calories~240 cal
Fat~12g
Carbohydrates~32g
Sugar~23g
Protein~3g

Allergens: Contains wheat, dairy, eggs. Not gluten-free. Not vegan.

3. Papa Johns Cinnamon Pull Aparts Full Review

Price: $7.29 | Calories: ~1,960 cal total (~245 per piece) | Serves: 2–4 people

The Cinnamon Pull Aparts are the most unique item on the Papa Johns dessert menu and in many ways, the most addictive. These are soft, bakery-style dough pieces dusted in cinnamon sugar and drizzled with a sweet white icing, served warm and designed to be pulled apart piece by piece.

What It Tastes Like

The dough is soft and pillowy the same quality fresh dough that Papa Johns uses for their pizza crust with a generous coating of cinnamon sugar on the outside and a warm, sweet icing drizzle that soaks into the dough slightly as it bakes. Each piece has a fluffy interior and a lightly crisped cinnamon exterior. The flavor is warm, comforting, and unmistakably cinnamon-forward without being overwhelming.

Why People Love It

The pull-apart format makes it uniquely social it is genuinely more fun to eat than a standard cookie or brownie. The dough quality is noticeably better than typical fast food cinnamon items because Papa Johns uses real, fresh-made dough. For anyone who loves cinnamon rolls, soft pretzels, or warm baked goods, the Cinnamon Pull Aparts are the standout on the Papa Johns dessert menu.

Best Way to Order It

Eat immediately these are best hot from the oven when the icing is still slightly warm and the dough is at peak softness. They do not reheat as well as the brownie or cookie. Order them only if you plan to eat them right away.

Nutrition Breakdown (full order):

Nutrient Amount
Calories~1,960 total
Per Piece~245 cal
Fat~7g per piece
Carbohydrates~40g per piece
Sugar~18g per piece

Allergens: Contains wheat, dairy. Check local menu for full allergen list.

4. Papa Johns Oreo Cookie Papa Bites Full Review

Price: $6.99 | Calories: ~100 cal per roll | Serves: 1–3 people

The Oreo Cookie Papa Bites are the most playful item on the Papa Johns dessert menu and at $6.99 for 8 rolls, also the most affordable. These are Papa Johns' fresh dough formed into small rolls, filled with an Oreo-inspired filling, baked until soft, and drizzled with a sweet cream cheese-style icing.

What It Tastes Like

The combination of Papa Johns' signature soft dough and the Oreo cookie filling creates something genuinely unexpected a warm, gooey, cookies-and-cream flavor in a pull-apart format. The icing drizzle adds extra sweetness and a creamy contrast to the chocolate Oreo filling. Each roll is about two bites, making them easy to snack through.

Why People Love It

They are the lightest dessert on the menu at 100 calories per roll, they are the easiest to justify compared to the brownie or cookie. They are also the most fun to eat, and the Oreo flavor combination is genuinely surprising in the best way. For kids especially, the Oreo Cookie Papa Bites are consistently the most-ordered dessert on family pizza nights.

Best Way to Order It

These work best as a lighter dessert after a big pizza order, or as a fun snack for younger family members. They are best eaten warm but hold their texture slightly better than the Cinnamon Pull Aparts if there are leftovers.

Nutrition Breakdown (per roll):

Nutrient Amount
Calories~100 cal
Fat~3g
Carbohydrates~16g
Sugar~7g
Protein~2g

Allergens: Contains wheat, dairy, soy. Contains Oreo (wheat, dairy, soy).

Papa Johns Desserts Compared Which One Should You Order?

Warm, bakery-style comfort foodCinnamon Pull Aparts
Lightest option / fun for kidsOreo Cookie Papa Bites
Best value for moneyOreo Cookie Papa Bites ($6.99)
Best for sharing 4+ peopleChocolate Chip Cookie or Brownie

How to Save Money on Papa Johns Desserts

1. Papa Rewards: Every dessert order earns Papa Dough points toward free food. Join the Papa Rewards program for free and earn on every order.

2. Bundle deals: Papa Johns frequently bundles a dessert with a large pizza order at a discounted combo price. Always check for active bundle deals before ordering separately.

3. Promo codes: Desserts are included in sitewide percentage-off promo codes. A 25% off code on a $25 order saves you nearly $2 on your dessert alone. Check Papa Johns coupons before every order.

4. App-only deals: The Papa Johns app occasionally offers exclusive dessert deals not available on the website. Download the app and enable notifications for the best dessert prices.

Papa Johns Desserts Calories & Nutrition Summary

For anyone managing their intake, here is the full calorie comparison across all Papa Johns desserts:

Lowest calorie choice: Oreo Cookie Papa Bites at 100 cal per roll by far the lightest option.
Highest calorie choice: Double Chocolate Chip Brownie at 240 cal per square but also the most satisfying for chocolate lovers.

For complete nutrition and allergen details, see our Papa Johns Nutrition Guide.

Final Verdict Are Papa Johns Desserts Worth Ordering?

Absolutely and the price makes the decision easy. At $6.99–$7.29 per dessert order serving 2–4 people, Papa Johns desserts represent excellent value for a freshly baked sweet treat.

The Chocolate Chip Cookie is the safest choice for groups. The Double Chocolate Chip Brownie is the right call for serious chocolate lovers. The Cinnamon Pull Aparts are the most unique and social experience on the menu. The Oreo Cookie Papa Bites are the best choice for kids or when you want something lighter.

Whatever you choose, order it warm and eat it fresh. That is when Papa Johns desserts are at their absolute best.

Planning your next Papa Johns order but not sure what's on the menu or how much it costs? You've come to the right place. This complete papa johns menu guide covers every single item available in 2026 — from their signature pizzas and crowd-pleasing wings to the newer Papadias, Papa Bites, sides, desserts, drinks, and dipping sauces — all with up-to-date prices.

Last Updated: March 2026

Whether you're ordering for yourself, feeding a family, hunting for the best Papa Johns deals, or just curious about what Papa Johns has to offer, this guide gives you everything you need in one place. We've also included calorie information, a breakdown of all current crust types, the best current deals, and tips on how to get the most value from your order.

Let's explore the full Papa Johns menu — slice by slice. 🍕

Why Papa Johns? A Quick Brand Overview

Papa Johns was founded in 1984 by John Schnatter in Jeffersonville, Indiana — starting out of a converted broom closet in his father's tavern. Fast forward to 2026, and Papa Johns is now the third-largest pizza chain in the United States, operating over 5,400 locations across more than 44 countries worldwide. You can find your nearest Papa Johns location using our store locator.

What makes Papa Johns stand apart from competitors like Domino's and Pizza Hut? Their core philosophy — 'Better Ingredients. Better Pizza.' — is more than just a slogan. Papa Johns uses fresh, never-frozen dough, 100% real mozzarella cheese, vine-ripened tomato sauce, and quality toppings with no artificial flavors or MSG in their standard recipes.

The result is a pizza that tastes noticeably fresher. And with a menu that has grown to include wings, flatbreads, bite-sized snacks, and more, Papa Johns is now a full meal destination, not just a pizza place. Make sure to check the Papa Johns Rewards program before you order to earn points on every purchase.

Whether you're a die-hard pepperoni fan, a wing lover, a vegetarian, or someone who just wants the most cheese possible — the Papa Johns menu in 2026 truly has something for everyone. With six crust options, over a dozen specialty pizzas, new Papa Bites, satisfying Papadias, and some of the best fast-food deals around, Papa Johns continues to deliver on its 'Better Ingredients. title: Pizza Delivery Near You: How to Get Hot Pizza Fast in 2026 (Complete Guide)
excerpt: Stop searching 'pizza near me' and discover why Papa John's is your top choice for fresh, hot, and speedy pizza delivery.
content:
Whether it's a lazy Friday night, a big game day, a birthday party, or you simply can't face cooking — pizza delivery near you is always just a few taps away. And in 2026, it has never been easier, faster, or more affordable to get a piping-hot pizza delivered straight to your door.

Last Updated: March 2026

But with so many pizza chains, delivery apps, and options available, how do you find the best pizza near you? How long will it take? Where are the best deals? And how do you make sure your pizza arrives hot and fresh every single time?

In this complete guide, we cover everything — from the top pizza delivery chains and how to find them, to insider tips, the best cheap deals, free delivery hacks, and exactly what to do for the fastest, most satisfying pizza delivery experience in 2026. Let's get that pizza ordered. 🍕

Why Pizza Delivery Is More Popular Than Ever in 2026

Pizza delivery is not just popular — it's a phenomenon. The search query 'pizza delivery near me' is one of the most searched food-related phrases in the entire world, with over 5 million searches every single month in the United States alone. That number has grown by a staggering 900% over the past five years, driven by the rise of online ordering, food delivery apps, and a permanent shift in how Americans eat at home.

The numbers tell the full story. According to the USDA, approximately 1 in 8 Americans eats pizza on any given day. About 59% of all pizza consumed in the U.S. is eaten at home — much of that delivered. And in 2026, 64% of consumers say they prefer placing pizza orders digitally. Why is pizza the king of delivery food? A few reasons stand out:

  • Universal appeal — pizza satisfies picky eaters, vegetarians, meat lovers, and adventurous foodies all at once
  • Easy customization — build the exact pizza you want with any toppings, crust, and sauce
  • Value for money — one pizza can feed a family for under $15–20
  • Fast delivery — average delivery time in 2026 is just 30 minutes and 32 seconds
  • Consistent quality — major chains maintain the same taste across thousands of locations

The pizza industry in 2026 is also evolving rapidly. Digital ordering, AI-powered phone support, and in-house delivery apps are replacing third-party platforms — and that means better prices and faster service for customers. Check the complete Papa Johns menu guide to see everything available for delivery right now.

Best Pizza Delivery Chains Near You in 2026

When it comes to pizza delivery near you, a handful of major chains dominate the landscape. Here's how the top three stack up in 2026, so you can choose the right one for your order. You can also find your nearest Papa Johns location instantly:

Feature Papa Johns ❤️ Domino's Pizza Hut
Avg Delivery Time~30 minutes ✅~28 minutes~35 minutes
Starting Price$10.99 (small)$7.99 (carryout)$10.99 (personal)
Ingredient QualityFresh, no MSG ✅StandardStandard
Crust Options6 crusts ✅5 crusts4 crusts
App ExperienceEasy + deals ✅Excellent ✅Good
Loyalty ProgramPapa Rewards ✅Domino's RewardsHut Rewards
Free Garlic SauceYes — always ✅NoNo
Order Accuracy97% ✅95%94%
Best ForQuality + valueSpeed + dealsVariety

💡 Our Pick: Papa Johns wins on ingredient quality, order accuracy (97%), free garlic sauce with every order, and the Papa Rewards loyalty program. For the best pizza delivery near you, Papa Johns is our top recommendation.

How to Find Pizza Delivery Near You — 4 Easy Methods

Method 1: Direct Chain Website or App (Best Option)

The best way to find pizza delivery near you is to go directly to the pizza chain's website or app. Just enter your delivery address, and the site instantly shows you whether your location is covered. This method typically offers the lowest prices and the best deals because there are no third-party fees added to your order.

  • Papa Johns: papajohns.com or Papa Johns app — enter address to see nearest location instantly
  • Domino's: dominos.com — their store locator is one of the fastest in the industry
  • Pizza Hut: pizzahut.com — shows all nearby locations with current hours

Method 2: Google Search (Fastest Discovery)

Type 'pizza delivery near me' directly into Google. Google will instantly show a map with the top pizza delivery options in your area, complete with ratings, hours, and distance. Click on any listing to see the menu, hours, and a direct link to order. This is perfect when you haven't decided which chain you want yet.

Method 3: Food Delivery Apps

Apps like DoorDash, Uber Eats, and Grubhub aggregate all pizza restaurants in your area into one interface. While convenient, these apps typically charge a delivery fee ($2–$5) and service fee (5–15%) on top of menu prices. Use them when your preferred chain doesn't offer its own delivery, or when you want to browse many restaurant options at once.

Method 4: Google Maps

Open Google Maps and search 'pizza delivery near me.' Google Maps shows you every pizza restaurant nearby with real-time hours, phone numbers, ratings, and photos. title: Papa Johns Papadias: All Flavors, Prices & Which One to Order in 2026
excerpt: Our famous flatbread sandwiches just got a massive upgrade. Explore the mouth-watering Papadias flavors launching this year.
content:
If you haven't tried Papa Johns Papadias yet, you're genuinely missing out on one of the most unique and satisfying items on their entire menu. Somewhere between a pizza and a flatbread sandwich — baked in Papa Johns' signature fresh dough, folded over generous, cheesy fillings, and served with a dipping sauce — the Papadia is unlike anything else from a major pizza chain.

Last Updated: March 2026

In this complete guide, we break down every single Papa Johns Papadia flavor available in 2026 — the original lineup, the Parmesan Crusted versions, and the Create Your Own option. We've included prices, calories, detailed taste reviews, allergen info, and our honest ranking of every flavor from worst to best, so you can order with total confidence.

Let's dig in.

What Is a Papa Johns Papadia?

The Papa Johns Papadia — pronounced 'pah-PAH-dee-ah' — is Papa Johns' take on the Italian piadina, a traditional flatbread sandwich originating from the Emilia-Romagna region of Northern Italy. According to food historians, the piadina has been a staple of humble Italian cooking for centuries, born as an affordable, filling meal made from simple dough ingredients.

Papa Johns introduced their modern version to the U.S. menu in February 2020, and the timing couldn't have been more interesting — just weeks before the global lockdowns began. Despite (or perhaps because of) the timing, Papadias became an instant hit and have remained a permanent fixture on the Papa Johns menu ever since.

What makes a Papadia different from a regular pizza? A few key things:

  • Shape: Folded flatbread instead of a round pizza — more like a pressed sandwich
  • Dough: Same fresh, never-frozen Papa Johns pizza dough — but baked flatter and crispier
  • Size: One Papadia is a single-person serving — no sharing needed
  • Experience: Handheld, portable, less messy than pizza — perfect for lunch or a quick meal
  • Comes with: A dipping sauce chosen to complement each specific flavor

Papa Johns describes it as 'a delicious pizza sandwich calzone combo.' However you categorize it, the Papadia delivers big flavor in a convenient, handheld format at a very fair price.

All Papa Johns Papadia Flavors & Prices (2026)

In 2026, Papa Johns offers nine distinct Papadia options — four original flatbread versions, four Parmesan Crusted versions, and the Create Your Own option. Here's the complete list:

Papadia Flavor Type Price Calories Dipping Sauce
Philly CheesesteakOriginal$7.99~800 calSpecial Garlic Sauce
BBQ Chicken & BaconOriginal$7.99~830 calBBQ Sauce
Grilled Buffalo ChickenOriginal$7.99~910 calRanch Dipping Sauce
ItalianOriginal$7.99~940 calPizza Dipping Sauce
Parmesan Crusted Philly CheesesteakParm ✨$8.99~970 calSpecial Garlic Sauce
Parmesan Crusted BBQ Chicken & BaconParm ✨$8.99~1,000 calBBQ Sauce
Parmesan Crusted Buffalo ChickenParm ✨$8.99~1,070 calRanch Dipping Sauce
Parmesan Crusted ItalianParm ✨$8.99~1,070 calPizza Dipping Sauce
Create Your Own PapadiaCustom$7.99~800 calYour choice

💡 Quick Tip: Original versions are $1 cheaper than Parmesan Crusted. All serve one person. Use our Papa Johns promo codes to save even more on your order!

Every Papa Johns Papadia Flavor — Detailed Reviews

1. Philly Cheesesteak Papadia — The Crowd Favorite

🏆 Rating: 4.2 / 5 | Best for: Steak & cheese lovers

The Philly Cheesesteak Papadia is the flagship of the entire Papadia lineup — and Papa Johns knows it, often using it as the hero image for the product. Inspired by the iconic Philadelphia cheesesteak sandwich, this Papadia is filled with sliced steak, onions, green bell peppers, Philly sauce, and melted mozzarella cheese, all folded inside fresh-baked flatbread and served with garlic dipping sauce.

The flavor profile is savory and satisfying — the Philly sauce has a distinctive, tangy richness that ties everything together. The steak is tender, the peppers add a fresh crunch, and the onions soften beautifully during baking. While it doesn't quite replicate the authentic Philadelphia cheesesteak experience, as a fast-food flatbread sandwich it's genuinely impressive.

  • Filling: Sliced steak, green peppers, onions, Philly sauce, mozzarella
  • Texture: Crispy outside, chewy inside, gooey melted cheese throughout
  • Taste: Savory, tangy, meaty — the Philly sauce is the star
  • Dipping Sauce: Special Garlic — a great pairing that adds richness
  • Verdict: Best overall Papadia. Order this if it's your first time.

2. Italian Papadia — The Hidden Gem

⭐ Rating: 4.4 / 5 | Best for: Italian food fans, adventurous eaters

The Italian Papadia is, in our opinion, the most underrated item on the entire Papa Johns menu. Inspired by Italian charcuterie traditions, it's filled with Alfredo sauce, Italian sausage, salami, banana peppers, and a mozzarella-provolone cheese blend — all baked to golden perfection and served with pizza dipping sauce.

The combination of creamy Alfredo, salty Italian sausage, tangy salami, and the sharp kick of banana peppers creates a complex, layered flavor that genuinely feels gourmet for a fast-food item. The banana peppers are the secret weapon here — they cut through the richness of the sauce and cheese, adding a bright, tangy note that keeps every bite interesting.

  • Filling: Alfredo sauce, Italian sausage, salami, banana peppers, mozzarella-provolone
  • Texture: Crispy crust, generous cheese pull, slightly chewy sausage
  • Taste: Rich, savory, tangy — the banana peppers make this special
  • Dipping Sauce: Pizza Sauce — surprisingly perfect contrast to the Alfredo base
  • Verdict: The best Papadia for flavor complexity. A must-try for Italian food lovers.

💡 Pro Tip: If you find banana peppers too overpowering, ask to reduce them when ordering. This makes the Alfredo and sausage flavors shine even more.

3. Grilled Buffalo Chicken Papadia — The Spicy Pick

🌶️ Rating: 4.0 / 5 | Best for: Spice lovers, buffalo wing fans

The Grilled Buffalo Chicken Papadia is Papa Johns' homage to the beloved buffalo wing — in flatbread sandwich form. Packed with grilled chicken, onions, a three-cheese blend, buffalo sauce, and buttermilk ranch sauce, this Papadia delivers a bold, tangy heat that's satisfying without being overwhelming.

What's impressive about this Papadia is how the flatbread crust holds up despite the double sauce (buffalo + ranch). It stays crispy and intact, not soggy — a real engineering achievement in fast-food bread construction. The grilled chicken is well-seasoned and tender, and the combination of spicy buffalo and cooling ranch is a classic for good reason.

  • Filling: Grilled chicken, onions, three-cheese blend, buffalo sauce, ranch
  • Texture: Stays crispy despite heavy saucing — impressive crust integrity
  • Taste: Spicy, tangy, with cooling ranch notes — bold and addictive
  • Dipping Sauce: Ranch — doubles down on the creamy cooling element
  • Verdict: Best Papadia for heat seekers. Also the highest calorie original option at ~910 cal.

4. BBQ Chicken & Bacon Papadia — The Sweet & Smoky

🍗 Rating: 3.8 / 5 | Best for: BBQ lovers, smoky flavor fans

The BBQ Chicken & Bacon Papadia combines grilled chicken, crispy bacon, onions, melted cheese, and BBQ sauce in a toasted flatbread, served with additional BBQ sauce for dipping. The concept is solid, and fans of BBQ chicken pizza will find familiar, satisfying flavors here.

The main critique of this Papadia is that the BBQ sauce can be quite heavy and sweet, sometimes overwhelming the chicken and bacon. If you're a big BBQ sauce fan, this is perfect. If you prefer more balanced flavors, the Philly or Italian Papadias may suit you better. The bacon is a nice touch — adding a salty, smoky counterpoint to the sweet sauce.

  • Filling: Grilled chicken, bacon, onions, cheese, BBQ sauce
  • Texture: Standard crispy flatbread, tender chicken, chewy bacon
  • Taste: Sweet, smoky, savory — BBQ sauce forward
  • Dipping Sauce: BBQ Sauce — an extra dose of the same sauce
  • Verdict: Best for BBQ lovers. Can feel one-note for others.

Parmesan Crusted Papadias — Are They Worth the Extra $1?

Papa Johns introduced the Parmesan Crusted Papadia as a premium upgrade to their original lineup. For $1 more ($8.99 vs $7.99), you get the same fillings but the outside of the flatbread is coated in a layer of blended Parmesan cheese before baking — creating a golden, nutty, crispy cheese crust on the outside.

Factor Original Papadia ($7.99) Parmesan Crusted ($8.99)
Price$7.99$8.99 (+$1)
CrustStandard flatbread — lightly crispedGolden parmesan coating — extra crunch ✅
TextureChewy + crispyCrispier, nuttier, more complex ✅
Calories800–940 cal970–1,070 cal (adds ~130 cal)
OilinessNormalSlightly oilier from cheese
FlavorBold, sandwich-forwardSame + savory cheesy richness ✅
VerdictPerfect for budget-conscious ordersWorth it for cheese lovers ✅

Our verdict? The Parmesan Crusted version is genuinely worth the extra dollar — especially on the Italian and Buffalo Chicken flavors, where the cheesy exterior adds a beautiful textural contrast. The BBQ Chicken Parmesan Crusted is the only one where the upgrade is debatable, as the sweet BBQ sauce and parmesan crust compete rather than complement each other.

💡 Best Parmesan Crusted Combo: Go Parmesan Crusted on Italian or Buffalo Chicken Papadia — these two flavors benefit most from the extra cheese crust.

Best Papa Johns Papadia Flavors — Ranked #1 to #5

Based on flavor complexity, texture, value, and customer reviews, here is our definitive ranking of the best Papa Johns Papadia flavors in 2026:

Rank Flavor Rating Why It Wins
#1Italian (Parmesan Crusted)4.5 / 5 ⭐Most complex flavor. Alfredo + sausage + banana peppers = gourmet surprise. Parmesan crust takes it over the top.
#2Philly Cheesesteak (Original)4.2 / 5 ⭐The crowd favorite for good reason. Classic, satisfying, consistently well-made. Perfect intro to Papadias.
#3Buffalo Chicken (Parmesan Crusted)4.0 / 5 ⭐Bold, spicy, and the crispy parmesan crust balances the saucy filling perfectly. Best for heat lovers.
#4BBQ Chicken & Bacon (Original)3.8 / 5 ⭐Solid, crowd-pleasing. Best if you love BBQ flavor. Can be one-note for those who prefer savory complexity.
#5Create Your OwnVariesThe most flexible option. Best when you know exactly what you want. Unlimited customization potential.

Papa Johns Papadia vs Pizza — Which Should You Order?

Both are made with the same fresh Papa Johns dough and quality ingredients — but they serve different occasions and cravings. Here's how to decide:

Factor Papadia Papa Johns Pizza
Price$7.99–$8.99 ✅ (single person)$10.99–$22.99 (1-4+ people)
Calories800–1,070 cal (entire item)180–390 cal per slice
PortabilityExcellent — handheld ✅Messy if eating on the go
Best ForSolo lunch, quick meal, on-the-goGroups, family dinners, sharing
CustomizationLimited (4 set flavors + custom)Unlimited toppings + 6 crusts ✅
Flavor VarietyBold, sandwich-style fillingsClassic pizza flavor profile ✅
Crust ExperienceCrispy, folded flatbreadRound pizza crust — more variety
Choose If...Eating alone, want something differentFeeding 2+ people, want classic pizza

Papa Johns Papadias Calories & Nutrition (2026)

Each Papadia is a single-serving item. Here's the complete nutritional breakdown for all available flavors. For more complete info on the whole menu, refer to our Papa Johns nutrition guide:

Papadia Calories Fat Carbs Protein Sodium
Philly Cheesesteak~800~38g~76g~40g~2,700mg
BBQ Chicken & Bacon~830~35g~83g~42g~2,600mg
Grilled Buffalo Chicken~910~45g~78g~44g~3,100mg
Italian~940~50g~76g~49g~3,000mg
Parm Crusted Philly~970~48g~78g~49g~2,900mg

Parm Crusted Buffalo Chicken~1,070~62g~77g~50g~3,200mg
Parm Crusted Italian~1,070~62g~76g~49g~3,010mg

💡 Lowest Calorie Pick: Philly Cheesesteak (original) at ~800 cal is your best bet if you're watching calories.

💪 Highest Protein: All Papadias deliver 40-50g protein per serving — excellent for high-protein meal plans.

⚠️ Sodium Warning: All Papadias are high in sodium (2,600–3,200mg). Not ideal for low-sodium diets — drink plenty of water!

Papa Johns Papadia Allergen Information

All Papa Johns Papadias are made with wheat-based flatbread dough, making them unsuitable for anyone with celiac disease or serious gluten allergies. Here's the full allergen overview:

  • Wheat / Gluten — Present in ALL Papadias (the flatbread dough contains wheat)
  • Milk / Dairy — Present in ALL Papadias (mozzarella, provolone, Parmesan cheese)
  • Soy — May be present in sauces and marinades depending on location
  • Eggs — Present in some sauces (ranch dipping sauce contains eggs)
  • No Tree Nuts — Not a standard ingredient in any Papadia
  • Sesame — Check with your local store; sesame regulations vary by region

⚠️ Important: Papadias are cooked in shared ovens. Cross-contamination with other allergens is possible. If you have a severe allergy, always call your local Papa Johns before ordering. You can use our store locator to find their number.

How to Order a Papadia from Papa Johns + Best Dipping Sauce Tips

How to Order Step-by-Step:

  • Visit papajohns.com or open the Papa Johns app
  • Navigate to 'Papadias' in the menu section
  • Choose your flavor — Original ($7.99) or Parmesan Crusted ($8.99)
  • Select 'Create Your Own' if you want custom fillings
  • Add any extras (extra sauce, extra cheese, sides, drinks)
  • Apply any promo code at checkout — check our Papa Johns coupons page first!
  • Place your order for delivery or carryout

Best Dipping Sauce Pairing Guide:

Papadia Flavor Included Sauce Best Bonus Sauce Pairing
Philly CheesesteakSpecial GarlicRanch or BBQ sauce — adds creamy or smoky dimension
ItalianPizza SauceGarlic Parmesan sauce — doubles the Italian richness
BBQ Chicken & BaconBBQ SauceRanch — cuts the sweetness, adds cool creaminess
Grilled Buffalo ChickenRanchBlue Cheese — classic buffalo pairing, extra tang
Create Your OwnYour choiceChoose based on your sauce selection during building

💡 Pro Tip: Reheat leftover Papadias in a pan or air fryer — never microwave. A few minutes at 350°F in an oven or 2 minutes in an air fryer restores the crispy crust perfectly.

Final Thoughts — Which Papa Johns Papadia Should You Order?

Whether you're a Papadia veteran or ordering for the first time, there's a perfect flavor waiting for you on the 2026 Papa Johns Papadias menu. First-timers should start with the classic Philly Cheesesteak — it's the crowd favorite for a reason. Adventurous eaters should go straight for the Italian, especially the Parmesan Crusted version. Spice lovers won't be disappointed by the Grilled Buffalo Chicken, and BBQ fans will enjoy every smoky, savory bite of the BBQ Chicken & Bacon.

Whatever you choose, don't forget to check for a promo code before you order — Papa Johns regularly offers 25-50% off deals that apply to the entire menu.

For first-timers, the Philly Cheesesteak Papadia is the most popular and consistently well-reviewed choice.

faqs.question: How many calories are in a Papa Johns Papadia?
faqs.answer: Papa Johns Papadia calories range from approximately 800 calories (Philly Cheesesteak original) to 1,070 calories (Parmesan Crusted Buffalo Chicken or Italian). Each Papadia is a single-serving item designed for one person. The original versions average around 870 calories, while the Parmesan Crusted versions average around 1,000 calories due to the added cheese coating. All Papadias are also high in protein, delivering 40-50g per serving.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a Papadia and a calzone?
faqs.answer: While both are made with pizza dough folded over fillings, there are key differences. A traditional calzone is a sealed, doughy pocket with fillings inside, baked until puffy and soft — typically larger and more filling-heavy. A Papa Johns Papadia is flatter, crispier, and more like a pressed flatbread sandwich than a puffy pocket. The Papadia is also served open-face or thinly folded rather than fully sealed, giving it a much crispier crust than a classic calzone.

faqs.question: Is the Papa Johns Papadia gluten-free?
faqs.answer: No. All Papa Johns Papadias are made with wheat-based flatbread dough and are not suitable for people with gluten intolerance or celiac disease. Papa Johns does not currently offer a gluten-free Papadia option. If you have a gluten sensitivity, Papa Johns offers a Gluten-Free Crust on their pizzas — however, this is also baked in shared ovens and is not recommended for people with celiac disease.

faqs.question: Can I customize my Papa Johns Papadia?
faqs.answer: Yes! Papa Johns offers a 'Create Your Own Papadia' option for $7.99 (or $8.99 for Parmesan Crusted). You can choose from a range of sauces (pizza sauce, Alfredo, BBQ, ranch), proteins (grilled chicken, Italian sausage, salami, steak), vegetables (onions, banana peppers, green peppers, mushrooms), and cheese blends. This option is perfect if you have specific preferences or want to experiment with flavor combinations not available in the standard lineup.

faqs.question: Can I get a Papa Johns Papadia delivered?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Papa Johns Papadias are available for both delivery and carryout. You can order through the Papa Johns website at papajohns.com, the Papa Johns mobile app, or by calling your nearest location. They are also available through third-party delivery apps like DoorDash, Uber Eats, and Grubhub depending on your area. For the freshest, crispiest Papadia, carryout is recommended as the crust can soften slightly during delivery.

title: Papa Johns Classic Pizzas: Every Flavor Ranked, Reviewed & Priced (2026)
excerpt: Explore all Papa Johns classic pizzas in 2026 — Pepperoni, Cheese, Sausage & more. Full prices, calories, honest reviews & which one to order. Complete guide inside!
content:
Before stuffed crusts, specialty flatbreads, and loaded meats pizzas came along — there were the Papa Johns classic pizzas. And here's the truth: after more than 40 years, the classics are still the most ordered items on the entire menu. The Pepperoni Pizza alone has been Papa Johns' best-selling pizza every single year since the chain launched in 1984.

Last Updated: March 2026

In this complete 2026 guide, we break down every Papa Johns classic pizza — Pepperoni, Cheese, Sausage, and Create Your Own — with detailed reviews, up-to-date prices, calorie counts, a full toppings list, crust options, and our honest ranking of which one to order. Whether you're a first-timer or a loyal Papa Johns fan, this guide tells you everything you need before placing your next order.

Let's get into it. 🍕

What Makes Papa Johns Classic Pizzas Different?

Papa Johns has built their entire reputation on one simple promise — 'Better Ingredients. Better Pizza.' And when it comes to their classic pizza lineup, that philosophy shows up in every single ingredient:

  • Fresh dough — made fresh daily at every Papa Johns location, never frozen or pre-made
  • 100% real mozzarella cheese — made from pasteurized milk with no fillers, blends, or imitation cheese
  • Vine-ripened tomato sauce — slow-cooked from real tomatoes with garlic, olive oil, and Italian herbs
  • No MSG — none of Papa Johns' standard ingredients contain monosodium glutamate
  • Quality toppings — pepperoni made from pork and beef, Italian sausage with real herbs and spices

This commitment to quality ingredients is why Papa Johns pizza review scores consistently outperform competitors in blind taste tests. The crust is noticeably fresher, the cheese melts more richly, and the sauce has a genuine tomato flavor that you simply don't find at most fast-food pizza chains.

The Papa Johns original pizza lineup is deliberately simple — and that simplicity is a strength. These are the pizzas that have satisfied millions of customers for over four decades, and they continue to be the foundation of everything Papa Johns does.

All Classic Pizzas + Complete Price Table

Papa Johns offers four core classic pizzas in 2026, available in four Papa Johns pizza sizes with multiple crust options. Here is the complete pricing guide:

Classic Pizza Small (6 slices) Medium (8 slices) Large (8 slices) XL (10 slices)

Pepperoni Pizza$10.99$12.99$15.78 ✅$16.99
Sausage Pizza$10.99$12.99$14.99 ✅$16.99
Create Your Own$10.99+$12.99+$14.99+ ✅$16.99+

💡 Best Value: Large pizza is the sweet spot — same 8 slices as medium but more generous size. Add a Papa Pairings deal to get even better value per slice.

📌 Price Note: Prices may vary slightly by location. Always check papajohns.com for your exact local pricing before ordering.

Papa Johns Pepperoni Pizza — Deep Review

🏆 Rating: 4.5 / 5 | #1 Best Seller | Best For: Everyone

If there's one pizza that defines Papa Johns — it's the Papa Johns pepperoni pizza. This is the most ordered pizza in the entire chain's history, and for very good reason. Made with Papa Johns' fresh hand-tossed dough, vine-ripened tomato sauce, 100% real mozzarella cheese, and generous slices of premium pepperoni, this is classic American pizza done right.

What sets this Papa Johns pepperoni pizza review apart from competitors? The pepperoni. Papa Johns uses a higher-quality pepperoni blend made from pork and beef with a distinct combination of spices that creates the signature slightly spicy, savory, smoky flavor. The pepperoni slices are also cut slightly thicker than industry standard — meaning you get a real meaty bite in every slice, not just a thin wafer of processed meat.

  • Sauce: Vine-ripened original pizza sauce
  • Cheese: 100% real mozzarella + Parmesan-Romano blend
  • Topping: Premium pork-and-beef pepperoni slices
  • Best Crust: Original Hand Tossed (classic) or Thin Crust (lighter)
  • Best Size: Large — best value per slice and most generous topping coverage
  • Pairs Well With: Garlic knots, extra garlic dipping sauce, Cheesesticks

Pepperoni Pizza — Calories Per Slice (Large, Original Crust):

Crust Type Calories Fat Carbs Protein
Thin Crust (large)~200 cal ✅~9g~22g~9g
Original Crust (large)~300–350 cal~14g~30g~13g

Epic Stuffed Crust~390–480 cal~20g~34g~18g

💡 Pro Tip: Order thin crust pepperoni if you're watching calories — same great flavor at nearly half the calories of stuffed crust!

Papa Johns Cheese Pizza — Deep Review

⭐ Rating: 4.3 / 5 | Best For: Purists, kids, sharing, pizza nights

The Papa Johns cheese pizza is the ultimate test of any pizza chain's quality — because when there's nothing to hide behind, every ingredient has to perform. No toppings, no distractions. Just dough, sauce, and cheese. And Papa Johns passes this test with flying colors.

What makes it stand out is the cheese itself. Papa Johns uses a blend of 100% real mozzarella made from whole milk, combined with a Parmesan-Romano blend that adds a subtle, nutty depth of flavor you don't get with standard mozzarella alone. The result is a cheese pizza with an unusually rich, complex flavor for what is essentially the simplest pizza on the menu. If you love cheese, ordering a Papa Johns extra cheese pizza is a must.

The Cheese Pizza is also the most customizable starting point — if you want to add toppings, starting from cheese gives you full control over the flavor profile. Many regular customers use the Cheese Pizza as their 'Create Your Own' base.

  • Sauce: Vine-ripened original pizza sauce
  • Cheese: 100% real mozzarella + Parmesan-Romano blend — generous coverage
  • No Toppings: Pure, unadulterated cheese pizza
  • Best Crust: Original Hand Tossed for maximum cheese pull, or NY Style for extra-large slices
  • Best For: Kids, pizza parties, cheese lovers, customization base
  • Extra Cheese Option: Add extra mozzarella for $1.50 — worth every penny

Cheese Pizza — Prices & Calories:

Papa Johns Sausage Pizza — Deep Review

⭐ Rating: 4.1 / 5 | Best For: Italian food lovers, savory flavor seekers

The Papa Johns sausage pizza is, in our opinion, the most underrated classic pizza on the entire menu. While pepperoni gets all the glory, the Sausage Pizza quietly delivers one of the most satisfying, flavor-forward eating experiences in the Papa Johns lineup.

Papa Johns uses a premium Italian-style pork sausage that is seasoned with a proprietary blend of herbs and spices — including fennel, black pepper, paprika, and Italian seasoning. The result is a sausage with a distinctly bold, aromatic flavor that pairs beautifully with Papa Johns' vine-ripened tomato sauce and rich mozzarella cheese. The sausage is also crumbled rather than sliced, which means better coverage across the pizza and a more consistent flavor in every bite.

  • Sausage Type: Premium Italian-style pork sausage — crumbled for even coverage
  • Flavor Profile: Savory, herby, with fennel and black pepper notes
  • Best Crust: Original Hand Tossed — the soft crust complements the bold sausage
  • Calories per slice (large, original crust): ~320 cal
  • Best For: Italian food fans, anyone who loves bold, savory flavors
  • Versus Pepperoni: Sausage is milder in spice but richer in herb flavor — equally satisfying

💡 Insider Tip: Try the Sausage Pizza with Extra Cheese and a drizzle of garlic butter sauce — this combination elevates it to an entirely different level.

Papa Johns Create Your Own Pizza — Complete Guide

The Papa Johns create your own pizza is exactly what it sounds like — a blank canvas where you choose every single element of your pizza. Starting at $10.99 for a small, you pick your crust, sauce, cheese level, and up to any combination of available Papa Johns pizza toppings.

Available Sauces (Choose 1):

  • Original Pizza Sauce — the classic, vine-ripened tomato base
  • Garlic Parmesan Sauce — creamy, rich, and buttery
  • BBQ Sauce — sweet and smoky, great with chicken or bacon
  • Buffalo Sauce — spicy and tangy, pairs well with chicken
  • Alfredo Sauce — Italian cream sauce, pairs with veggies and chicken

Available Meats & Vegetables (Each ~$1.50 extra):

  • Meats: Pepperoni, Italian Sausage, Grilled Chicken, Bacon, Beef, Anchovies, Ham, Salami
  • Veggies: Mushrooms, Green Bell Peppers, Black Olives, Onions, Banana Peppers, Tomatoes, Spinach, Jalapeños, Pineapple, Roasted Garlic

Papa Johns Pizza Sizes — Which Size Is Right for You?

All Crust Options for Classic Pizzas

All Papa Johns classic pizzas are available in all six Papa Johns pizza crust types. Here's which crust works best with each classic pizza:

Papa Johns vs Domino's Classic Pizza — Honest Comparison

💡 Our Verdict: For overall pizza quality, ingredient freshness, and value — Papa Johns pizza vs dominos is an easy win for Papa Johns. For the absolute cheapest carryout price, Domino's has the edge. But when you factor in the free garlic sauce, better pepperoni, and fresher dough, Papa Johns delivers more value per bite.

Final Thoughts — The Papa Johns Classic Pizza Is Still the Best

After 40+ years, the Papa Johns classic pizza lineup remains as relevant, satisfying, and delicious as ever. The Pepperoni is the undisputed best-seller for good reason — but don't sleep on the Sausage Pizza or the pure simplicity of the Cheese. Whatever you choose, make sure you check for a promo code before ordering and join Papa Rewards if you haven't already.

faqs.question: What are the classic pizzas at Papa Johns?
faqs.answer: Papa Johns classic pizzas include four core offerings: Cheese Pizza, Pepperoni Pizza, Sausage Pizza, and Create Your Own Pizza. These are the foundational menu items that have been available at Papa Johns since the chain launched in 1984. They are available in small, medium, large, and extra-large sizes, with all six crust types, and are priced from $10.99 for a small to $16.99+ for an extra-large.

faqs.question: How much does a Papa Johns pepperoni pizza cost?
faqs.answer: A Papa Johns Pepperoni Pizza costs $10.99 for a small, $12.99 for a medium, $15.78 for a large, and $16.99 for an extra-large (original crust). faqs.question: How many calories are in a Papa Johns pepperoni pizza?
faqs.answer: One slice of Papa Johns pepperoni pizza calories (large, original crust) count is approximately 300-350 calories. An entire large pepperoni pizza (8 slices) contains around 2,400-2,800 calories total. Choosing thin crust reduces calories to approximately 200 calories per slice. The Epic Stuffed Crust version contains the most calories at approximately 390-480 calories per slice depending on size.

faqs.question: How many slices are in a Papa Johns pizza?
faqs.answer: Papa Johns pizzas are cut into different numbers of slices depending on size. A small pizza is cut into 6 slices, a medium pizza is cut into 8 slices, a large pizza is cut into 8 slices, and an extra-large pizza is cut into 10 slices. Note that the large and medium are both cut into 8 slices — but the large slices are noticeably bigger because the pizza itself is 2 inches larger in diameter. So when asking how many slices in papa johns pizza, always remember to factor in the size of the slice.

faqs.question: What is the best classic pizza at Papa Johns?
faqs.answer: Based on overall popularity, taste, and value, the Papa Johns Pepperoni Pizza is considered the best papa johns pizza on the menu. It has been Papa Johns' best-selling pizza every year since the chain launched in 1984. For those who prefer a milder flavor, the Cheese Pizza is an excellent choice — Papa Johns uses a superior cheese blend that makes their cheese pizza stand out from competitors. The Sausage Pizza is our top pick for anyone who enjoys bold, herby Italian flavors.

faqs.question: Can I customize a classic Papa Johns pizza?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Papa Johns classic pizzas are fully customizable. You can add any of the available toppings, change the sauce, adjust the cheese level (light, normal, or extra), choose from all six crust types, and request a well-done bake for a crispier crust. You can also start with the Cheese Pizza as a base and add any combination of toppings to effectively create your own pizza at the classic pizza price point.

faqs.question: What crust is best for Papa Johns classic pizzas?
faqs.answer: For the Pepperoni Pizza, the Original Hand Tossed crust is the classic choice — but the Thin Crust is an excellent lower-calorie option. For the Cheese Pizza, the New York Style crust creates an authentic, large-slice experience. The Epic Stuffed Crust pairs especially well with Pepperoni — the cheese-filled edge and pepperoni topping create an indulgent combination. For the Sausage Pizza, the Garlic Epic Stuffed Crust pairs beautifully with the Italian herb sausage.

faqs.question: Is Papa Johns classic pizza better than Domino's?
faqs.answer: In terms of ingredient quality, Papa Johns classic pizza generally outperforms Domino's. Papa Johns uses fresh, never-frozen dough made daily, thicker-cut pepperoni with a premium pork-and-beef blend, 100% real mozzarella with a Parmesan-Romano addition, and vine-ripened tomato sauce without added sugar. Papa Johns also includes free garlic dipping sauce and pepperoncini with every order. Domino's has an advantage in delivery speed and sometimes in carryout pricing, but for overall pizza quality and ingredient freshness, Papa Johns is the stronger choice.

title: Papa Johns Super Loaded Pizzas: All Flavors & Prices 2026
excerpt: Discover every Papa Johns Super Loaded Pizza in 2026 — The Works, The Meats, BBQ Chicken Bacon & more. Full prices, calories, honest reviews & ordering tips inside.
content:
If you've ever stared at a regular pizza and thought "this needs more" — you're exactly who Papa Johns built the Super Loaded Pizzas for. These aren't your average pies. We're talking edge-to-edge toppings, premium meats stacked generously, fresh-cut vegetables, and enough cheese to make any ordinary pizza feel underdressed.

Last Updated: March 2026

In this complete 2026 guide, we break down every Super Loaded Pizza on the Papa Johns menu — including full prices by size, calories per slice, honest flavor reviews, and the best ways to order them. Whether you're feeding a crowd or treating yourself, this is the only guide you need.

What Are Papa Johns Super Loaded Pizzas?

Super Loaded Pizzas are Papa Johns' specialty pizza lineup — the highest-tier, most topping-packed options on the entire menu. While Classic Pizzas like Pepperoni or Cheese are crowd-pleasers, Super Loaded Pizzas are built for those who want maximum flavor in every bite.

What sets them apart:

  • More toppings than any Classic Pizza — up to 6 premium ingredients per pizza
  • Higher-quality cuts — julienne-cut Canadian bacon, fresh-cut onions, crisp green peppers
  • Every slice is loaded — no thin spots, no bare edges
  • Available on any crust — Original, Thin, New York Style, or Epic Stuffed Crust

These pizzas start at $14.99 and range up to $23.99 depending on size. Yes, they cost more than Classic Pizzas — but the difference in toppings and flavor density makes every extra dollar count.

If you're new to Super Loaded Pizzas, start here.

Which Super Loaded Pizza Should You Order?

Here's a quick guide based on what you're craving:

  • I want the classic experience → The Works. It's the benchmark against which all other loaded pizzas should be judged.
  • I don't eat vegetables → The Meats. Zero vegetables, maximum protein.
  • I want something lighter → Super Hawaiian. Fewest calories on the Super Loaded menu.
  • I want bold, smoky flavors → BBQ Chicken Bacon. The BBQ sauce base makes this unlike anything else on the menu.
  • I like spicy food → Fiery Buffalo Chicken. Real heat, balanced by the ranch drizzle.
  • I love cheese above all else → Fresh Tuscan Six Cheese. Six-cheese blend is genuinely impressive.

Ordering Tips for Super Loaded Pizzas

  1. Order a size up if you're sharing. Super Loaded Pizzas are heavy and filling — a Large comfortably feeds 3 people, but if you're adding sides, an Extra Large gives everyone more room.
  2. Add the Epic Stuffed Crust on The Works. This is the single best upgrade you can make. The cheese-stuffed crust completes the "more is more" philosophy of the entire Super Loaded category.
  3. Pair with Garlic Parmesan Breadsticks. The lighter, herby flavor of the breadsticks balances the intensity of a Super Loaded pizza better than any other Papa Johns sides & dips.
  4. Use Papa Rewards for free points. Every dollar you spend on Papa Johns earns Papa Dough through the Papa Rewards program. A Large Super Loaded Pizza earns enough points toward a future free side.
  5. Check for coupons before ordering. Super Loaded Pizzas occasionally appear in combo deals and weekly specials. Always check the latest Papa Johns coupons before placing your order — you can often get a Large Super Loaded for close to Classic Pizza prices.

Super Loaded Pizza Nutrition Guide

Managing your intake while enjoying Super Loaded Pizzas is very possible. Here's what to know:

  • Lowest calorie option: Super Hawaiian at 260 cal/slice (Large)
  • Highest calorie option: The Meats at 460 cal/slice (Extra Large)
  • Most protein-dense: The Meats (four types of protein per slice)
  • Vegetarian-friendly: Fresh Tuscan Six Cheese (no meat)

For full nutritional details including sodium, carbohydrates, and allergen information, visit the Papa Johns Nutrition Guide.

Final Verdict: Are Papa Johns Super Loaded Pizzas Worth It?

Short answer: yes, absolutely.

The price premium over Classic Pizzas is real, but so is the difference in eating experience. Super Loaded Pizzas are built for occasions when you want a pizza that actually impresses — whether that's a family dinner, a game-night order, or simply a moment when only maximum flavor will do.

If you've never tried one, start with The Works on the Original Crust with Epic Stuffed Crust upgrade. It's the definitive Super Loaded experience and the reason these pizzas have become one of the most-ordered categories on the entire Papa Johns menu.
